content.text: DARK COMEDY SCRIPT Title: “SORRY FOR YOUR LOSS (IT’S COMPLICATED)” Genre: Dark Comedy / Satire Estimated Duration: ~45–50 minutes FADE IN: EXT. CREMATORIUM PARKING LOT – MORNING [Approx. Timestamp: 00:00:00] Grey sky. Drizzle. A banner flaps loosely: “WELCOME – TODAY’S FUNERAL” Someone taped over “FUNERAL” with “CELEBRATION OF LIFE,” but it’s peeling. A hearse stalls. Engine coughs. Silence. INT. CREMATORIUM – WAITING HALL [00:02:30] Rows of plastic chairs. Awkward silence. Meet the mourners: DEV (34) – The dead man’s son, emotionally numb MAYA (32) – Dev’s ex-wife, overly polite UNCLE GIRISH (55) – Loud, inappropriate RITA (40) – Funeral coordinator who has seen too much A phone rings. Everyone stares. Dev answers. DEV Yes. He’s still dead. Hangs up. CUT TO: INT. WAITING HALL – CONTINUOUS [00:05:30] Rita checks her clipboard. RITA Just confirming… this is a sad funeral, right? DEV Define sad. RITA Okay. No balloons. She removes balloons awkwardly. CUT TO: EXT. CREMATORIUM BACK AREA [00:08:00] Uncle Girish smokes beside the coffin. GIRISH (to coffin) You always said you’d leave early. Ash falls on the coffin. GIRISH (CONT’D) Sorry. Wind. CUT TO: INT. HALL – EULOGY SETUP [00:11:00] Maya approaches Dev. MAYA I’m sorry. For everything. DEV You picked a bad day to mean that. They stand in silence. Someone accidentally starts HAPPY BIRTHDAY on the speaker. Rita PANICS. CUT TO: INT. CEREMONY HALL – FUNERAL STARTS [00:14:30] Dim lights. Heavy silence. Priest clears throat. PRIEST We are gathered to honor— Microphone SCREECHES. PRIEST (CONT’D) —yes, that’s loud. Everyone winces. CUT TO: INT. CEREMONY HALL – EULOGIES [00:17:30] Uncle Girish takes the mic. GIRISH He was a good man. Not great. But good enough. Dev stares. GIRISH (CONT’D) He borrowed money… and never returned it. A beat. GIRISH Death is closure. Scattered, uncomfortable laughter. CUT TO: INT. HALLWAY – BREAK [00:20:30] Dev leans against the wall. Rita joins him. RITA I’ve done this for fifteen years. DEV Does it get easier? RITA No. It just gets funnier in a way you feel guilty about. They share a small smile. CUT TO: INT. CREMATION ROOM – PREP [00:24:00] The coffin is pushed in. Machine BEEPS. TECHNICIAN Power cut. Silence. TECHNICIAN (CONT’D) We’ll resume when electricity comes back. Everyone just… waits. CUT TO: INT. CREMATION ROOM – DARK [00:27:30] Emergency lights flicker red. Uncle Girish whispers. GIRISH This is symbolic. DEV Please stop talking. CUT TO: INT. WAITING HALL –

POWER RETURNS [00:30:30] Lights BLAST back on. A slideshow auto-plays. Photos include: The deceased sleeping The deceased blinking mid-photo One photo of a random stranger MAYA Who’s that? DEV Wrong life. CUT TO: INT. CREMATION ROOM – FINAL [00:33:30] The machine starts. Low HUM. Dev watches. DEV (softly) You were never good at staying. CUT TO: EXT. CREMATORIUM – AFTERNOON [00:37:00] Ash urn handed over. Rita hands Dev a bill. RITA Would you like feedback form? DEV On my father? RITA On us. CUT TO: EXT. PARKING LOT – CONTINUOUS [00:40:00] Rain stops. Sunlight breaks through clouds — awkwardly cheerful. Maya looks at Dev. MAYA Coffee? DEV Sure. It’s not like today can get worse. A bird IMMEDIATELY poops on his shoulder. They both laugh. FINAL SHOT: EXT. COFFEE SHOP – EVENING [00:45:30] They sit quietly. Dev stirs coffee. DEV (V.O.) Grief isn’t loud. It just trips you when you’re trying to stand. They sip. Silence. FADE TO BLACK. END.
